text: Add a UI surface for autonomy policy and runtime ownership
description: Expose .bowman/runtime/autonomy-policy.json and related runtime state in the frontend so the user can inspect and adjust autonomy mode, lease defaults, reconnect behavior, and commit-before-compact rules without editing files manually.

reflections.context: Expose autonomy policy and runtime ownership in the frontend so users can inspect and adjust durable autonomy state without editing files manually.
reflections.outcome: Extended the project settings modal with autonomy policy controls and a runtime ownership section that displays runtime paths and current per-agent runtime state from backend-owned .bowman/runtime files.
reflections.effectiveness: This was a good first UI step because it reused an existing settings surface instead of adding another panel. That kept the change small while making the new runtime files visible and editable.
reflections.lesson: For early autonomy features, integrate them into familiar project settings first. It creates visibility quickly and avoids premature UI sprawl.
reflections.pattern: This follows the same pattern as other release/autonomy work here: move durable state into backend/file ownership first, then expose a thin UI surface on top.

status.comment: Added a first autonomy UI surface inside Project Settings. It now exposes autonomy-policy.json controls and shows current runtime ownership/status from .bowman/runtime.
